steps.title: Spot the confirmation
steps.content: The linguist assumes the word "own" appears often. The corpus shows it is the eighth most commonly used adjective — confirming, not contradicting, that assumption. The blank describes what a linguist could do to an assumption using corpus data. It must mean to confirm using evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean to confirm or support with evidence. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — validate: means to confirm as true using evidence — the corpus data (eighth most common adjective) directly confirms the linguist’s assumption that "own" appears often. ✓
  • Choice B — refute: means to disprove — the corpus confirms the assumption, it does not disprove it; ranking eighth most common supports rather than refutes frequency. ✗
  • Choice C — transpose: means to rearrange or swap the position of elements — irrelevant; corpus data doesn’t rearrange an assumption, it tests it. ✗
  • Choice D — entail: means to involve or imply as a necessary consequence — an assumption doesn’t entail data; data validates assumptions. The logical relationship is wrong. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
